title: Star Wars (2015) 47
content:
{{Top|rwm|aor|title=''Star Wars'' (2015) 47}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars 47}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:StarWars2015-47.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'' 47 |writer=[[Kieron Gillen]] |penciller=[[Salvador Larroca]] |inker=Salvador Larroca |letterer=[[Clayton Cowles]] |colorist=[[Guru-eFX]] |cover artist=*[[David Marquez]] *[[Matthew Wilson]] |editor=[[Mark Paniccia]] |publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] |release date=[[May 2]], [[2018]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/67036/star_wars_2015_47|text=Star Wars (2015) #47}} |pages=23 |upc=759606081134 |timeline=[[1 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars Book X: Mutiny at Mon Cala]]'' to [[1 ABY]]. |series=[[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'' (2015)]] |issue=47 }} '''''Star Wars'' 47''' is the forty-seventh issue of the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Kieron Gillen]] and illustrated by [[Salvador Larroca]]. It was released on [[May 2]], [[2018]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''The Rebellion's fight to liberate Mon Cala continues as Han, Luke and Leia undertake a perilous and nigh-impossible rescue.'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Part IV
MUTINY AT MON CALA |text=It is a period of rebuilding in the galaxy. After the victory on Jedha, thanks to Queen Trios' secret betrayal of the Empire, Rebel leaders Princess Leia, Luke Skywalker and Han Solo search for a new base and new allies in their struggle against the evil Galactic Empire. Meanwhile, Trios continues to feign loyalty to the Empire while on the Imperial-occupied water-world, Mon Cala. With the Mon Cala king Lee-Char imprisoned, his people are hesitant to add their powerful fleet to the rebel cause. But Leia has a plan to free him and win their support. With the Moff of Mon Cala captured and replaced with a shapeshifting Clawdite, Leia, Luke and Han plan to use their prisoner to free another....}} ==Plot summary== At the [[Moncaladrome]] on [[Mon Cala]], [[Queen]] [[Trios]] and [[Dors Urtya]] grow impatient with the [[Moff]] [[Tan Hubi]]'s uncharacteristic tardiness. When he finally arrives, [[C-3PO]] notes that the Hubi seems far too happy for the somber occasion, as he watches from the side of the theater while remarking on the tragic tale of the "[[Faluvian cycle]]" opera. Unbeknownst to everyone at the opera house, Hubi is being impersonated by the [[Changeling|shapeshifter]] [[Tunga Arpagion]]. Meanwhile, underneath the [[ocean]] of [[Strokill Prime]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|rebels]] are in trouble, as a massive [[fish]] tries to eat their [[Submarine|submersible]]. [[Han Solo]] questions why it would want to eat a metal ship, and [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] explains it's likely targeting their bright lights. [[Luke Skywalker]] puts on a suit and gets out a device called the Sled. Solo turns the lights of the submersible off, and Skywalker distracts the massive fish with his lightsaber before [[Chewbacca]] rescues him with the Sled at the last possible moment. Back at the opera house, during the intermission, Urtya and Queen Trios discuss "Hubi's" surprising behavior. The rebels work their way back into the Moncaladrome with the real Hubi in tow. Upon learning that they are trying to rescue [[King]] [[Lee-Char]], the Hubi reveals that the machines they hooked him up to are the only things keeping him alive. ==Media==
